topic: A person's online friend turns out to live in the same city
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a familiar chime that usually brought a fleeting sense of connection, but today it just grated on my nerves. Liam. He’d been a phantom in my digital life for months, a source of vapid, overly cheerful updates that I found grating. Then, a mutual acquaintance mentioned a shared zip code. My stomach clenched. I clicked the profile, my fingers hovering over the screen before I finally scrolled down. The city listed under his location was mine. A cold knot tightened in my chest. I reread his recent posts, the bright sunshine in his photos suddenly feeling like an unwelcome spotlight. I typed a reply, my thumbs trembling slightly, aiming for something sharp, something to push him away, anything to reestablish the distance I’d painstakingly built.

[story 2]

I stared at the screen, the blue light reflecting off my tired eyes. It was impossible. Liam, the perpetually distant voice in my DMs, was actually just a few subway stops away. The realization hit me like a physical blow, stealing the air from my lungs. I felt a sudden, intense heat rise in my neck, and I instinctively pulled my phone away, gripping it so tightly my knuckles turned white. What was I supposed to do with this intrusion? The casual banter I’d exchanged suddenly felt like a performance, a flimsy shield against the reality that he was physically present, breathing the same air. I began to pace my small apartment, the sound of my own footsteps echoing in the silence, a restless energy churning beneath my skin.

[story 3]

The message arrived, unannounced, simply stating he was near the downtown library. I felt a sharp spike of annoyance, a tightening around my jaw that I couldn't suppress. I checked the map, a slow, deliberate movement. He was three blocks from my building. A wave of irritation washed over me, making my breath catch in my throat. I didn't want to see him. I didn't want to acknowledge the shared geography. I stood by the window, watching the city bustle below, feeling intensely closed off, every muscle in my body coiled tight with a need to retreat and ignore the undeniable proximity of this online acquaintance.
